findings.message: create_item is wired to a second, parallel data-access module (db/item_service.add_item) while the route already depends on db/repository (list_items, MAX_PAGE_SIZE). db/repository.py already exposes a create_item(session, name) that performs the same add/commit/refresh. Introducing db/item_service as a duplicate data layer for the same Item model splits persistence logic across two modules, creating inconsistent transaction semantics (item_service.add_item only flushes, so the route must commit/refresh by hand, whereas repository.create_item owns the full unit of work) and inappropriate coupling: the routes layer now reaches into two competing data-access seams.
findings.suggestion: Drop the db/item_service import and call the existing repository.create_item (which already does session.add + commit + refresh), removing the manual session.commit()/session.refresh() in the handler. Consolidate all Item persistence into db/repository.py and delete the redundant db/item_service.py so there is a single data-access layer.
